seed: vulnerability vs strength; exposure_vs_shelter; rising_signal; outside_inward; flood against spark; hard_edges; thin_vs_heavy; tiny_plus; connection_alienation | abstract variation request 345; target batch count 500
title: Echoes of Contrast
reasoning: This composition captures the tension between vulnerability and strength through a deliberate arrangement of color and pixel structure. The palette, titled 'ancient_fire', comprises burnt orange and dark red to suggest intensity and urgency, while charcoal provides grounding depth. Gold adds a hint of brightness, representing moments of hope amidst struggle. The layout, 'open_gate', symbolizes exposure, with a diagonal rise that invites an energetic flow from the bottom left to the top right, signifying strength rising against vulnerability. The main contrast occurs around the center, where the lighter and softer colors of vulnerability collide with the surrounding stronger, darker edges, emphasizing a struggle against confinement. Broken pixels introduce elements of disruption that speak to the imperfect nature of both emotional states, while maintaining negative space allows for reflection and clarity within the chaos.

layout.name: open_gate
layout.description: A frame or wall opens on one side, letting a contrasting flow enter.

composition.name: center_vs_edges
composition.idea: The center is protected or attacked by the surrounding pixels.

referenceExample.title: Broken Love
referenceExample.pattern: paired shapes with one side corrupted

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
